reasoning: The Lean formalization precisely mirrors the informal statement. The hypothesis `∑ i ∈ range (n+1), (C i)/(i+1) = 0` unfolds to $C_0/1 + C_1/2 + \cdots + C_n/(n+1) = 0$, matching the informal hypothesis exactly. The conclusion `∃ x ∈ Set.Ioo 0 1, ∑ i ∈ range (n+1), (C i)*(x^i) = 0` captures "the polynomial $C_0 + C_1 x + \cdots + C_n x^n$ has a real root strictly between 0 and 1" — `Set.Ioo 0 1` is the open interval $(0,1)$. The coefficients are typed `ℕ → ℝ`, and although defined on all of ℕ, only indices 0..n contribute, matching "$C_0, \ldots, C_n$ are real constants". No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure is preserved.
